name: page-constructor-builder
description: CLI tool for building static HTML pages from YAML configurations using @gravity-ui/page-constructor with webpack, watch mode, custom components, navigation, and analytics.
when_to_use: CLI tool for building static HTML pages from YAML configurations using @gravity-ui/page-constructor with webpack, watch mode, custom components, navigation, and analytics
avoid: Not suitable for dynamic server-rendered applications — output is purely static HTML. Not for development-mode translation workflows.

name: page-constructor-builder
description: CLI tool for building static HTML pages from YAML configurations using @gravity-ui/page-constructor. Supports custom React components, SCSS, navigation, analytics, favicon, watch mode, and per-locale asset manifests in a single webpack-based build pipeline.
when_to_use: Building static HTML pages from YAML content files using the Gravity UI page-constructor block system
Generating documentation or landing pages without a full SSR framework
When you need watch mode, custom components, navigation, and analytics in a zero-config static build
Prototyping pages visually defined in YAML with Gravity UI design tokens
avoid: Not suitable for dynamic server-rendered applications — output is purely static HTML
Does not support non-literal YAML expressions or dynamic data fetching at build time
how_to_use: Install with `npm install @gravity-ui/page-constructor-builder`. Add script `"build": "page-builder build"` to package.json. Create `page-builder.config.yml` specifying input/output directories, assets, theme, and optional navigation/css/components paths. Create YAML page files under the input directory with `meta` and `blocks` sections using @gravity-ui/page-constructor block types. Run `npm run build`.
